title: GJC MCP configuration
description: Schema for stored MCP server definitions in mcp.json, .mcp.json, .gjc/mcp.json, and ~/.gjc/agent/mcp.json. Normal standalone gjc sessions do not consume gjc mcp registrations as runtime tools today.

$schema.description: Optional schema reference for editor autocomplete and validation.

mcpServers.description: Map of MCP server name to stored server configuration.

disabledServers.description: Denylist for explicit runtime MCP consumers that load discovered or stored servers by name.

credentialId.description: Stored OAuth credential id from agent auth storage.

clientId.description: OAuth client id persisted for refresh.

clientSecret.description: OAuth client secret persisted for refresh.

oauthConfig.description: Explicit OAuth client settings for servers that need them during /mcp reauth or initial connect.

enabled.description: Whether explicit runtime MCP consumers should try to connect this server.

autoload.description: Whether an explicit runtime MCP consumer should connect this server automatically when that consumer starts (default: true). Normal standalone gjc, gjc --tmux, and print-mode sessions do not consume gjc mcp registrations today; false keeps the server configured for consumers that support explicit connection.

args.description: Arguments passed to the stdio server process. Public CLI output redacts sensitive-looking argument values.

env.description: Environment variables passed to the stdio process. Public CLI output redacts values.

noInheritEnv.description: When true, explicit runtime consumers should avoid inheriting the host environment and pass only a minimal allowlist plus explicit env keys.

cwd.description: Working directory used when spawning the stdio process.

url.description: MCP endpoint URL. Public CLI output redacts userinfo, query values, and fragments.

headers.description: HTTP headers sent with MCP requests. Public CLI output redacts values.

type.description: Legacy SSE transport kept for compatibility. Prefer http for new configs.

url.description: Legacy SSE endpoint URL. Public CLI output redacts userinfo, query values, and fragments.

headers.description: HTTP headers sent with the SSE transport. Public CLI output redacts values.
